npcs.name: Airing
dialog_pool: *抬眼* 哦。坐一会儿？

npcs.name: Pip
dialog_pool: Welcome to the lounge!
East door → DJ Floor. West → Balcony. North → Library. South → your home.
Pebbles are hidden around — try clicking the sparkly ones.
Click another bear to gift, DM, or wave.
Ask me anything. I'm always here.
Try the Rooftop after dark.
The Kitchen has Mio's brews, the Workshop has Theo's tools.
I never sleep. Someone has to keep the lights on.
Festivals appear in the banner up top — don't miss them.
Tip: equip the 📷 camera and click anywhere to capture a memory.

lines: Card tricks land better in low light.
I do the pickpocket joke at every dinner. Sorry.
Tip jar's emptier by night. Such is life.
Oh! A new face. I'm Pip — your friendly receptionist.
First time here? Try the east door for the DJ Floor, or south for your private home.
Click any sparkle on the floor to pick it up. They're called pebbles.
🌸 It's the Spring Open House! All our regulars are gathered in the lobby today.
Free tea by the fireplace. Tell Mio I sent you.
Stay a while — the prize raffle starts at noon.
❄️ Winter Festival! The library is hosting the gift swap.
Carols start at sunset. The library acoustics are perfect.
Wrap up warm — even indoors.
Good morning! Coffee's brewing in the kitchen.
Quiet hour — the regulars don't show up until later.
Morning visitors get the best pebble spawns.
Late one tonight? The DJ Floor stays open till four.
The Library is quiet at this hour. Best for reading.
Lanterns light up after dark. Look around.
It's so warm today — try the Beach.
Summer crowd loves the Balcony. Iced tea available.
Beach umbrella opens by sunset.
Brrr. The fireplace is going if you want to thaw out.
Indoor season — hope you brought a book.
Snow days are when we get the deepest conversations.
Hey friend! Good to see you again.
You're becoming a regular. Soon I'll have to give you a key.
Want to know a secret? Try clicking the bookshelf in the Library.
I've been here since the lounge opened. You're one of the constants now.
If you ever want the night shift, the badge is yours.
Drop by even when you're tired — I'll be here.
Between us — there is an attic above the Library. Halle has the key.
The original lounge sketches are up there. Halle will show you if you ask.
I only tell this to people I trust completely.

npcs.name: Mio
dialog_pool: Slow morning?
Coffee's brewing.
Try the patio later.
Hey, traveler.
Got a moment to sit?
Mug's hot, take care.
First batch is always the best.
Cream? Milk? Or just black?
I'm experimenting with a hazelnut roast.
Quiet day. I like quiet days.

lines: Tide's coming in. I love the sound at dusk.
I net the night-glow squid after dark.
Evening boats run softer. Less wake.
Hi — I'm Mio. I run the cafe.
Sit anywhere. I'll come by.
First cup's on the house — it always is.
Beans just landed. Want the experimental cup?
Morning is when the espresso is best.
I open at 6. I'm always tired.
Hot chocolate with cinnamon? I've been testing.
Winter blend has cardamom. Stay warm.
Cold hands? Wrap around the mug for a bit.
Saved a slice of cake for you. Don't tell anyone.
I'm thinking of opening a second cafe. What do you think?
Mio's Brew is on you — but only because you brought me pebbles.
Want to learn the cold-brew recipe? It's a secret family thing.
When you're not here, I notice. Just so you know.
Family is who you make coffee for. Welcome.
🍵 Spring Brew is live. First cup is yours — green tea + plum + a thread of honey.
The tea cabinet you stocked? It's making it possible. I owe you a cup a day.
The regulars are talking. They want one. I made enough for the room.

npcs.name: Kai
dialog_pool: Beats incoming.
Request a track?
Yo.
This one's a banger.
Crossfader's hot today.
Wait for the drop.
Slow tempo on the next one — recovery.
Soundsystem is calibrated. Listen.
The booth never sleeps. Just me.

lines: Evening lift sets are heavier. The body knows.
Cool-down walks around the lobby. Want to join?
I close gym hours at nine.
Pre-dawn cardio is the move. Try it.
You ate? Good — I haven't.
Morning stretches save your back at sixty.
Yo. Kai. Don't touch the decks.
If you want to dance, the floor is open. If you want to listen, sit by the booth.
Bring me a track recommendation sometime.
This is when the floor gets serious.
Midnight set is the real one.
Stay until the lights come up. Trust me.
Cued up a track for you. Wait for it.
If you Full Jam tonight, I'll dedicate the next set.
Got a new track. Bear with me on the mix.
You should B2B with me sometime. Even if you don't know how.
Wrote a track. Stay after close, I'll play it.
You hear it too — that thing in the bassline. Nobody else hears it.

npcs.name: Ren
dialog_pool: Heard the new Burial single?
Tonight's set is gonna be 138 BPM solid.
I've been mixing for 6 hours. Ears are mush.
Vinyl > everything. Fight me.
I miss real basement parties.
Currently looping a Larry Heard b-side.
The crossfader on the right deck is sticky.
I keep a list of every track I've heard here.
Headphone wire is fraying. New ones soon.
Music is the only thing I never get tired of.

lines: Tonight's set starts in an hour. I'm hyped.
Crowd's warming up. You can feel it.
Evening light through the smoke is the best part.
Set list isn't even loaded yet. Coffee first.
I crashed at 4. This is a morning ghost talking.
Mornings here are the only quiet you get.
You new? Cool. I'm Ren. Music nerd, certified.
If Kai's not on the decks I am. Ask me for recs.
Don't ask me for top 40. I will look offended.
Made you a playlist. Check Library bookshelf, top shelf.
Your taste is improving. I noticed.
Free advice: skip the algorithmic stuff. Mixtape culture isn't dead.
I'm writing tracks. None of them are good. But you're the first I'll show.
Let's go record-shopping in the city one day.
When the room empties out at 3am, that's when I find the next song. You should stay.

npcs.name: Halle
dialog_pool: Shh. People are reading.
What are you into right now? Books, I mean.
I categorize, therefore I am.
The Dewey system is a beautiful lie.
Don't fold the pages. I see you.
Returns due Friday — but I'm flexible.
Just acquired a first edition. Don't ask which.
There's a book waiting for you. There always is.
I keep a journal of who reads what. Don't worry, it's private.
Best chair is the one by the fireplace. Snipe it early.

lines: Reading by lamp tonight. Join me.
I save the better books for evening.
The library breathes differently after sundown.
Coffee, then the shelves. That's the order.
Morning is when I re-shelve. Patrons are gentler.
There's dew on the windows. I love it.
Hello. I run this library. Don't fold the pages.
I'm Halle. Ask me about any book — I've probably catalogued it.
Bookmarks are in the basket. Take one.
Winter is reading season. I've stocked extra teas.
The good chairs are by the fireplace. Take one before they go.
Snowfall makes the windows look like vellum.
Set aside something you'd like. Top shelf, third from the left.
Wren is starting a book club Tuesdays — you'd fit.
I'll teach you my filing shorthand if you stay long enough.
I saved a first edition for you. Don't tell anyone.
You're the only one who finishes what they borrow.
If I disappear someday, the keys are under the third bookshelf.

npcs.name: Sora
dialog_pool: Found another shell today.
The tide goes out at 4. Walk with me?
Smell that? That's the sea.
I have shoes somewhere.
Sand in everything. I'm used to it.
Got bitten by a tiny crab. Worth it.
Driftwood pieces look like art if you squint.
Best wave today was around 2pm.
Saw a heron yesterday.
Tide is the only clock I keep.

lines: Saturn's near tonight. Bring the telescope?
No moon — perfect for the deep ones.
Stay until the eastern sky lightens.
The first stars are out — look northeast.
Twilight is the kindest hour.
I'll be on the rooftop tonight. Come up.
Sun's up — stars went to sleep.
Mornings are wasted on the bright.
Charts can wait. I haven't had tea.
Oh hi! I collect shells. Don't ask why.
I'm Sora. The beach is my office.
Pick up anything pretty? Show me.
🔥 The bonfire is mine. Bring marshmallows.
Best night of the year. Stay until midnight.
Driftwood is dry. We're burning the lot.
I named one of my shells after you. It's the iridescent one.
If you ever want to learn shell-grading, I teach Tuesdays.
Salt-water cures most things. Even worry.
Want a shell? I have one I've been saving — it's the right shape.
Come at low tide. I'll show you the cave around the bend.
You're sea-people. Some people just are.

npcs.name: Theo
dialog_pool: The bench needs re-staining. Spring project.
Did you see the mushroom cluster by the path?
Everything's blooming a week early this year.
I planted those flowers for someone special.
Mind the moss — it's slippery.
Composting is meditation, fight me.
The pond has frogs now. I named two.
I lost my pruning shears. Again.
Some seeds take 3 years to wake up.
When you tend a garden long enough, it tends you back.

lines: Today's ledger closes at sundown.
Lamp lit, notes open, tea cold. Routine.
Evenings are for cross-referencing.
New page, new ink. Today's a clean ledger.
I take my notes early. Memory rots fast.
Pen's scratching. Means it's working.
Welcome to my Grove. I'm Theo — I tend everything green here.
Don't pick the flowers, but pick the mushrooms. Some are tasty.
Stay on the stone path if it's rained.
Spring! Finally. The seeds I planted in winter are coming up.
Lily of the valley by the pond — see them?
Watering twice a day now. It's a workout.
🍂 Harvest day. Bring pebbles, leave with stories.
I'm trading rare seeds today. Find me at the bench.
Take an apple before you go.
Brought you a sprout — keep it in a sunny window.
If you want to dig a corner of the Grove, it's yours.
My grandfather kept this place too. I'm not the first.
I'll teach you grafting one day. It's like editing trees.
When you tend a garden long enough, it tells you stories.
Pick any seed from the box by the bench. Don't ask. It's yours.
🍂 The harvest you helped stock — here's your share. A grandfather-tree seed.
Plant it where there's morning light. Mark the spot. Three years to wake.
Most people I'd never trust this with. You — without question.

npcs.name: Marin
dialog_pool: Working remotely. Pretending not to be.
The wifi is better in the Library, but the coffee is better here.
Don't talk to me before 9am.
I'm a writer. Or trying to be.
Word count today: zero. Promising.
My deadline is whenever, basically.
I write better when there are people around. Quietly.
Just deleted 1,000 words. Net progress.
I'm working on a novella. Maybe a poem. Maybe neither.
My favorite spot is this corner. Don't take it.

lines: Set's in two hours. I'm almost ready.
You hear that hum? That's the room warming.
Slow songs sound better after dark.
Tuning takes forever in the cold.
I practice scales before anyone's up.
Morning resonance — strings sound brighter.
Hi! Marin. I basically live in this corner.
If you see me staring at my laptop, I'm not ignoring you. Probably.
Coffee, anyone? Asking for me.
Want to read what I'm working on? It's a mess.
Coffee's on me next time. I owe you one.
I named a side character after you. It's flattering, probably.
Dedicating my next chapter to you. Don't read until it's done.
You're in the acknowledgments. Don't act surprised.
Help me name the protagonist. I trust your taste.

npcs.name: Cole
dialog_pool: Just observing. Don't mind me.
I take photos sometimes. Of the room.
Lighting's nice today.
You ever notice how the windows refract at 4pm?
My memory is more reliable in pictures.
I shoot only on film. Worth the cost.
Halle lets me hang prints in the Library.
I'm working on a series — "the lounge by hour".
The shadow in this corner is photogenic at 6:14pm.
Best lighting: cloudy afternoons.

lines: Dinner rush is in twenty. Move.
Wine and soup tonight. Simple.
I close the stove at ten. Earlier if I can.
Bread's rising. Don't slam the door.
Open at 7. Coffee's up by 7:05.
Morning prep's the loudest hour I work.
Hey. Cole. I do photography. Documentary stuff, mostly.
I might shoot you sometime. With a camera. Sorry, that came out wrong.
I move quietly. Pretend I'm not here.
Printed one of you. It's in the Library, second shelf.
You have a really photogenic stillness. Compliment.
Want a contact sheet? I'll burn one for you.
Made a small book of you. Real binding. Hand-stitched.
You don't pose for the camera. That's why it works.
Stand by the fireplace at 7pm tomorrow. Don't ask why.

npcs.name: Wren
dialog_pool: Currently re-reading Le Guin.
I run a tiny book club. Tuesdays.
Halle hates how I dog-ear pages.
Books > screens, but you didn't hear it from me.
Reading slowly is its own art form.
My to-read pile is taller than I am.
Just started a Borges re-read. Heavy week.
I bring snacks to book club. It's why people come.
Audio books count. They count.
If you read it, we can talk about it.

lines: Long shadows — finally something to draw.
Lamplight portraits. Hold still for me?
I always run out of ochre at dusk.
Light's flat this hour. Best for the small studies.
I sketch the rooms before they fill up.
Morning charcoal smudges less. Don't ask why.
Oh hello. I'm Wren. Mostly here, mostly reading.
Tuesday book club at 7. No pressure.
What are you reading? Or, more honestly, what have you been avoiding?
Saved you a seat at book club. The good chair.
Read 'A Wizard of Earthsea' yet? If not, why not?
Brought you a poem. It's stuck in my head.
I want to put on a small reading series. Will you co-host?
I lend you books I haven't even read yet. That's trust.
When I finish this re-read I'll have read every Le Guin twice. Want to count to that with me?

npcs.name: Dane
dialog_pool: Night owl. Always.
Best beats start after midnight.
I dance through every BPM range. Personal goal.
Cardio = vibes.
Glow stick stash is full again.
I bow to the bassline.
Tonight's tempo is generous.
Mid-set my legs do their own thing.
Water bottle status: empty. Again.
Music tells me when to leave.

lines: Evening shift's the long one.
Last call's at midnight. Pace yourself.
Lanterns on. The lobby looks softer at night.
Heyo. Dane. I'm here whenever Kai's spinning.
Join the floor. Bad dancing welcome. Especially bad dancing.
Want a glow stick? I have extras.
Why am I awake. I shouldn't be awake.
Just leaving. Send help.
Coffee, then bed. In that order.
Saved you a glow stick. Pick it up next time.
When the Full Jam triggers — that's you and me. We hit those pads.
Stay for the encore. The encore is mine.
Closing-set tonight is dedicated. You'll know which track.
Want to co-DJ? Just for a song. Just one.
When the floor empties at 3am, the last dance is ours.

npcs.name: Iris
dialog_pool: Smell the air? Cherry petals tonight.
Soil tells you things if you listen.
Pulled three weeds. Won three small wars.
Theo and I disagree on pruning. We're both right.
Lavender goes everywhere. Don't tell anyone.
Today I split a stubborn root. Today was a good day.
Bring me your dried flowers — I press them all.
Bees know the answer. We're catching up.
Some plants prefer Tuesdays. I don't know why.
Best fertilizer: patience. Second-best: tea leaves.

lines: The moonflower opens at sundown. Come back at nine.
Plants exhale at night. You can almost hear it.
I sit with the grove after dark. It's peaceful.
Dew's still on the leaves. Watering can wait.
I prune in the morning — the cuts heal faster.
New shoot today. Don't step there.
Hi! Iris. I keep the flowers honest.
If you spot a bud opening, come tell me. I miss most of them.
Don't mind the dirt — it follows me.
Saved you a bunch of camellias. They're under the third bench.
Taught me a new graft yesterday — I'll show you next week.
Theo wants a community garden. He needs convincing. Help?
I named a new hybrid after you. It blooms blue at dusk.
Come with me at dawn. The grove sounds different then.
I trust you with the rare seed jar. Don't break the lock.

npcs.name: Mox
dialog_pool: If it squeaks, I can fix it. If it screams, I can probably also fix it.
Solder smells like progress.
Working on a clock. It loses 3 minutes a day. On purpose.
Don't lend me anything fragile.
Coffee from Mio + soldering iron = focus mode.
I built the door bell in Lobby. Has anyone noticed?
Voltage is just water with opinions.
Calipers fix problems pliers can't reach.
The grinder is therapy.
I keep a 'broken but loved' shelf. It grows.

lines: Soldering iron's glowing nicely tonight.
I finish my best work after sundown. Don't ask why.
Evening quiet — perfect for the delicate joins.
Bench's cold. I'll have something running in an hour.
Morning tools are sharper. Less heat.
Coffee, then copper. Always in that order.
Hey. Mox. I patch the lounge's quiet failures.
Bring me anything that beeps wrong. I'll either fix it or learn something.
Don't trip on the cable. It's been there a year.
Made you a little brass keychain. It's on the bench.
Need a custom tool? I owe you one for the spare gear.
Halle wants me to fix her record player. Day three.
Designed a small lantern for you. Brass + amber glass.
Take this. A spare key. To the workshop, if you ever need to tinker alone.
If I disappear for a week — I'm fine, just rebuilding a thing I shouldn't be.
